items.title: Starbucks Data Breach Impacts Employees
items.description: Starbucks said the incident involved phishing attacks targeting an employee portal, affecting hundreds. The post Starbucks Data Breach Impacts Employees appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: In Other News: N8n Flaw Exploited, Slopoly Malware, Interpol Cybercrime Crackdown
items.description: Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: Telus Digital data breach, vulnerabilities in Linux AppArmor allow root privileges, US defense contractor behind Coruna exploits. items.title: Storm-2561 Spreads Trojan VPN Clients via SEO Poisoning to Steal Credentials
items.description: Microsoft has disclosed details of a credential theft campaign that employs fake virtual private network (VPN) clients distributed through search engine optimization (SEO) poisoning techniques. items.title: Fake enterprise VPN downloads used to steal company credentials
items.description: A threat actor tracked as Storm-2561 is distributing fake enterprise VPN clients from Ivanti, Cisco, and Fortinet to steal VPN credentials from unsuspecting users. [...]

items.title: Iran-Linked Hacker Attack on Stryker Disrupted Manufacturing and Shipping
items.description: Evidence indicates that the attackers leveraged existing endpoint management software rather than malware to wipe devices. The post Iran-Linked Hacker Attack on Stryker Disrupted Manufacturing and Shipping appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Onyx Security Launches With $40 Million in Funding
items.description: The startup is building a control pane to help organizations oversee autonomous AI agents and rapidly adopt them. The post Onyx Security Launches With $40 Million in Funding appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Google Fixes Two Chrome Zero-Days Exploited in the Wild Affecting Skia and V8
items.description: Google on Thursday released security updates for its Chrome web browser to address two high-severity vulnerabilities that it said have been exploited in the wild. The list of vulnerabilities is as follows - CVE-2026-3909 (CVSS score: 8.8) - An out-of-bounds write vulnerability in the Skia 2D graphic

items.title: Nine CrackArmor Flaws in Linux AppArmor Enable Root Escalation, Bypass Container Isolation
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed multiple security vulnerabilities within the Linux kernel's AppArmor module that could be exploited by unprivileged users to circumvent kernel protections, escalate to root, and undermine container isolation guarantees. items.title: Starbucks discloses data breach affecting hundreds of employees
items.description: Starbucks has disclosed a data breach affecting hundreds of employees after threat actors gained access to their Starbucks Partner Central accounts. [...]

items.title: Chrome 146 Update Patches Two Exploited Zero-Days
items.description: The flaws can be exploited to manipulate data and bypass security restrictions, potentially leading to code execution. The post Chrome 146 Update Patches Two Exploited Zero-Days appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Google fixes two new Chrome zero-days exploited in attacks
items.description: Google has released emergency security updates to patch two high-severity Chrome vulnerabilities exploited in zero-day attacks. [...]

items.title: Authorities Disrupt SocksEscort Proxy Botnet Exploiting 369,000 IPs Across 163 Countries
items.description: A court-authorized international law enforcement operation has dismantled a criminal proxy service named SocksEscort that enslaved thousands of residential routers worldwide into a botnet for committing large-scale fraud. "SocksEscort infected home and small business internet routers with malware,"

items.title: Veeam Patches 7 Critical Backup & Replication Flaws Allowing Remote Code Execution
items.description: Veeam has released security updates to address multiple critical vulnerabilities in its Backup & Replication software that, if successfully exploited, could result in remote code execution. The vulnerabilities are as follows - CVE-2026-21666 (CVSS score: 9.9) - A vulnerability that allows an authent

items.title: Canadian retail giant Loblaw notifies customers of data breach
items.description: Still, out of an abundance of caution, Loblaw says it has automatically logged out all customers from their accounts. Account holders who need to access the company's digital services will have to log in again. [...]

items.title: England Hockey investigating ransomware data breach
items.description: England Hockey, the governing body for field hockey in England, is investigating a potential data breach after the AiLock ransomware gang listed it as a victim on its data leak site. [...]

items.title: Rust-Based VENON Malware Targets 33 Brazilian Banks with Credential-Stealing Overlays
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new banking malware targeting Brazilian users that's written in Rust, marking a significant departure from other known Delphi-based malware families associated with the Latin American cybercrime ecosystem. items.title: Google paid $17.1 million for vulnerability reports in 2025
items.description: Google paid over $17 million to 747 security researchers who reported security bugs through its Vulnerability Reward Program (VRP) in 2025. [...]

items.title: Apple Updates Legacy iOS Versions to Patch Coruna Exploits
items.description: The company has released iOS and iPadOS versions 16.7.15 and 15.8.7 to patch the vulnerabilities. The post Apple Updates Legacy iOS Versions to Patch Coruna Exploits appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Telus Digital confirms breach after hacker claims 1 petabyte data theft
items.description: Canadian business process outsourcing giant Telus Digital has confirmed it suffered a security incident after threat actors claimed to have stolen nearly 1 petabyte of data from the company in a multi-month breach. [...]

items.title: Apple patches older iPhones and iPads against Coruna exploits
items.description: ​Apple has released security updates to patch older iPhones and iPads against a set of vulnerabilities targeted in cyberespionage and crypto-theft attacks using the Coruna exploit kit. [...]
